```{r}
plot(simulateResiduals(testmod)) #all good

testmod <- lm(Dim.3~Species*Basin, data= pca.df2)
plot(simulateResiduals(testmod)) #fails outlier test; will use glmm

testmod <- lm(CatPC1~Species*Basin, data= catpca.df2)
plot(simulateResiduals(testmod)) #all good

testmod <- lm(CatPC2~Species*Basin, data= catpca.df2)
plot(simulateResiduals(testmod)) #fails KS test, outlier test, and combined adjusted quantile test; will use glmm


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## MEDIAN TESTS (GLMM for all)
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

### Metric Data
geo2.met.mean.1 <- glmmTMB(Dim.1 ~Species*Basin, dispformula = ~Species * Basin, data = pca.df2)
(g2mm1 <- Anova(geo2.met.mean.1, type = "III")) #basin and species:basin significant (p<0.001) but not species
plot(simulateResiduals(geo2.met.mean.1)) #all good

    post_hoc2.1 <- emmeans(geo2.met.mean.1,  ~ Species | Basin)
    summary(post_hoc2.1)
    pw2.1 <- pairs(post_hoc2.1)
    (pair2.1 <- as.data.frame(rbind(pw2.1, adjust = "holm"))) #significant species difference in San Antonio-Nueces Coastal (p<0.001), Upper and Lower Southwest Texas coastal (p=0.003 and p<0.001) and Rio Tamesi (p=0.014)

geo2.met.mean.2 <- glmmTMB(Dim.2 ~Species*Basin, dispformula = ~Species*Basin, data = pca.df2) 
(g2mm2 <- Anova(geo2.met.mean.2, type = "III")) #all effects significant (all p<0.001)
plot(simulateResiduals(geo2.met.mean.2)) #all good

    post_hoc2.2 <- emmeans(geo2.met.mean.2,  ~ Species | Basin)
    summary(post_hoc2.2)
    pw2.2 <- pairs(post_hoc2.2)
    (pair2.2 <- as.data.frame(rbind(pw2.2, adjust = "holm"))) #all Basins significant (p<0.001)


geo2.met.mean.3 <- glmmTMB(Dim.3 ~Species*Basin, dispformula = ~Species*Basin, data = pca.df2) 
(g2mm3<- Anova(geo2.met.mean.3, type = "III")) #all effects significant (p<0.001)
plot(simulateResiduals(geo2.met.mean.3)) #all good

    post_hoc2.3 <- emmeans(geo2.met.mean.3,  ~ Species | Basin)
    summary(post_hoc2.3)
    pw2.3 <- pairs(post_hoc2.3)
    (pair2.3 <- as.data.frame(rbind(pw2.3, adjust = "holm"))) #only San Antonio-Guadalupe (p<0.001) and Lower Southwest Texas coastal (p=0.003) significant

### Meristic Data
geo2.cnt.mean.1 <- glmmTMB(CatPC1 ~Species*Basin, dispformula = ~Species*Basin, data = catpca.df2)
(g2cm1<- Anova(geo2.cnt.mean.1, type = "III")) # species (p<0.001) and species:Basin (p=0.013) significant, but not Basins
plot(simulateResiduals(geo2.cnt.mean.1)) #all good

    post_hoc2.4 <- emmeans(geo2.cnt.mean.1,  ~ Species | Basin)
    summary(post_hoc2.4)
    pw2.4 <- pairs(post_hoc2.4)
    (pair2.4 <- as.data.frame(rbind(pw2.4, adjust = "holm"))) #all Basins significant (p<0.001)

    
#default gaussian family still failed residual tests; switching to Student-t distribution due to extreme tail ends
geo2.cnt.mean.2 <- glmmTMB(CatPC2 ~Species*Basin, dispformula = ~Species*Basin, family= t_family, data = catpca.df2)
(g2cm2<- Anova(geo2.cnt.mean.2, type = "III")) #basin (p<0.001) and species:basin (p=0.037) significant, but not species
plot(simulateResiduals(geo2.cnt.mean.2)) #shows quantile deviations for 0.75 but a n.s. adjusted quantile test and aligned qqplot

    post_hoc2.5 <- emmeans(geo2.cnt.mean.2,  ~ Species | Basin)
    summary(post_hoc2.5)
    pw2.5 <- pairs(post_hoc2.5)
    (pair2.5 <- as.data.frame(rbind(pw2.5, adjust = "holm"))) #no Basins significant

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## VARIANCE TESTS (Brown-Forsythe for all)
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

### Metric Data
geo2.met.var.1 <- leveneTest(Dim.1~Species*Basin, data= pca.df2) #significant (p<0.001)
    post_hoc2.6 <- emmeans(geo2.met.mean.1,  ~ Species | Basin, component = "disp")
    summary(post_hoc2.6)
    pw2.6 <- pairs(post_hoc2.6)
    (pair2.6 <- as.data.frame(rbind(pw2.6, adjust = "holm"))) #only Upper SW Texas Coastal (p<0.001) and Lower Rio Grande (p=0.049) significant


geo2.met.var.2 <- leveneTest(Dim.2~Species*Basin, data= pca.df2) #not significant
   
    
geo2.met.var.3 <- leveneTest(Dim.3~Species*Basin, data= pca.df2) #marginal (p=0.050)
    post_hoc2.7 <- emmeans(geo2.met.mean.3,  ~ Species | Basin, component = "disp")
    summary(post_hoc2.7)
    pw2.7 <- pairs(post_hoc2.7)
    (pair2.7 <- as.data.frame(rbind(pw2.7, adjust = "holm"))) #only Lower SW Texas Coastal significant (p<0.001)
    

### Meristic Data
geo2.cnt.var.1 <- leveneTest(CatPC1~Species*Basin, data= catpca.df2) #not significant

geo2.cnt.var.2 <- leveneTest(CatPC2~Species*Basin, data= catpca.df2) #not significant


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## TABLE
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

### Metric Data

#get medians and median absolute deviations for each dimension per species per Basin
geo2_met_summary <- pca.df2 %>%
  group_by(Species, Basin) %>%
  summarise(
    PC1_median = median(Dim.1, na.rm = TRUE),
    PC1_mad  = mad(Dim.1, na.rm = TRUE, constant = 1),
    PC2_median = median(Dim.2, na.rm = TRUE),
    PC2_mad  = mad(Dim.2, na.rm = TRUE, constant = 1),
    PC3_median = median(Dim.3, na.rm = TRUE),
    PC3_mad  = mad(Dim.3, na.rm = TRUE, constant = 1))

#format summary stats
geo2_met_long <- geo2_met_summary %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("PC"),
    names_to = c("Dimension", ".value"),
    names_pattern = "(PC\\d)_(.*)") %>%
  mutate(stat_string = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", median, mad)) %>%
  select(Species, Basin, Dimension, stat_string) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Species, values_from = stat_string)


#extract Anova(model) results and levene's results
stats_results2.1 <- tibble(
  Dimension = c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"),
  Chi_value = c(
    g2mm1["Species:Basin", "Chisq"], 
    g2mm2["Species:Basin", "Chisq"], 
    g2mm3["Species:Basin", "Chisq"]),
  p_int     = c(
    g2mm1["Species:Basin", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
    g2mm2["Species:Basin", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
    g2mm3["Species:Basin", "Pr(>Chisq)"]),
  F_value   = c(
    geo2.met.var.1$`F value`[1], 
    geo2.met.var.2$`F value`[1], 
    geo2.met.var.3$`F value`[1]),
  p_lv      = c(
    geo2.met.var.1$`Pr(>F)`[1], 
    geo2.met.var.2$`Pr(>F)`[1], 
    geo2.met.var.3$`Pr(>F)`[1]))


#format results for final table
postPCA_geo2_result <- geo2_met_long %>%
  left_join(stats_results2.1, by = "Dimension") %>%
  mutate(
    Classifier = "Metric PCA",
    Med_test = case_when(
      p_int < 0.001 ~ paste0("&chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chi_value), "<br>*p* < 0.001*"),
      p_int < 0.05  ~ paste0("&chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chi_value), "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_int), "*"),
      TRUE          ~ paste0("&chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chi_value), "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_int))),
    Variance_test = case_when(
      p_lv < 0.001 ~ paste0("F = ", sprintf("%.2f", F_value), "<br>*p* < 0.001*"),
      p_lv < 0.05  ~ paste0("F = ", sprintf("%.2f", F_value), "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_lv), "*"),
      TRUE          ~ paste0("F = ", sprintf("%.2f", F_value), "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_lv))))




### Meristic Data


#get medians and median absolute deviations for each dimension per species per Basin
geo2_cnt_summary <- catpca.df2 %>%
  group_by(Species, Basin) %>%
  summarise(
    CatPC1_median = median(CatPC1, na.rm = TRUE),
    CatPC1_mad  = mad(CatPC1, na.rm = TRUE, constant = 1),
    CatPC2_median = median(CatPC2, na.rm = TRUE),
    CatPC2_mad  = mad(CatPC2, na.rm = TRUE, constant = 1))

#format summary stats
geo2_cnt_long <- geo2_cnt_summary %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("CatPC"),
    names_to = c("Dimension", ".value"),
    names_pattern = "(CatPC\\d)_(.*)") %>%
  mutate(stat_string = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", median, mad)) %>%
  select(Species, Basin, Dimension, stat_string) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Species, values_from = stat_string)


#extract Anova(model) results and levene's results
stats_results2.2 <- tibble(
  Dimension = c("CatPC1", "CatPC2"),
  Chi_value = c(
    g2cm1["Species:Basin", "Chisq"], 
    g2cm2["Species:Basin", "Chisq"]),
  p_int     = c(
    g2cm1["Species:Basin", "Pr(>Chisq)"], 
    g2cm2["Species:Basin", "Pr(>Chisq)"]),
  F_value   = c(
    geo2.cnt.var.1$`F value`[1], 
    geo2.cnt.var.2$`F value`[1]),
  p_lv      = c(
    geo2.cnt.var.1$`Pr(>F)`[1], 
    geo2.cnt.var.2$`Pr(>F)`[1]))


#format results for final table
postCPCA_geo2_result <- geo2_cnt_long %>%
  left_join(stats_results2.2, by = "Dimension") %>%
  mutate(
    Classifier = "Meristic CatPCA",
    Med_test = case_when(
      p_int < 0.001 ~ paste0("&chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chi_value), "<br>*p* < 0.001*"),
      p_int < 0.05  ~ paste0("&chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chi_value), "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_int), "*"),
      TRUE          ~ paste0("&chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chi_value), "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_int))),
    Variance_test = case_when(
      p_lv < 0.001 ~ paste0("F = ", sprintf("%.2f", F_value), "<br>*p* < 0.001*"),
      p_lv < 0.05  ~ paste0("F = ", sprintf("%.2f", F_value), "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_lv), "*"),
      TRUE          ~ paste0("F = ", sprintf("%.2f", F_value), "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_lv))))



### Add posthoc test results
extract_pairs2 <- function(pair_obj, dim_name, type = "Med") {
  if (is.null(pair_obj)) return(NULL)
  
  pair_obj %>%
    mutate(
      Dimension = dim_name,
      Basin = as.character(Basin),
      p_fmt = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "*p* < 0.001*",
        p.value < 0.05  ~ sprintf("*p* = %.3f*", p.value),
        TRUE            ~ sprintf("*p* = %.3f", p.value)),
      PostHoc_Sum = sprintf("<b>%s:</b> %.2f (&plusmn;%.2f); %s", type, estimate, SE, p_fmt)) %>%
    select(Dimension, Basin, PostHoc_Sum)
}

# Bind all pairwise comparisons together
posthoc_ref2 <- bind_rows(
  extract_pairs2(pair2.1, "PC1", "M"),
  extract_pairs2(pair2.2, "PC2", "M"),
  extract_pairs2(pair2.3, "PC3", "M"),
  extract_pairs2(pair2.4, "CatPC1", "M"),
  extract_pairs2(pair2.5, "CatPC2", "M"),
  extract_pairs2(pair2.6, "PC1", "V"),
  extract_pairs2(pair2.7, "PC3", "V")) %>%
  group_by(Dimension, Basin) %>%
  summarise(
    PostHoc_Summary = paste(PostHoc_Sum, collapse = "<br>"),
    .groups = "drop")


### Combine & form table

geo2_results_combined <- bind_rows(postPCA_geo2_result, postCPCA_geo2_result) %>%
  mutate(
    Med_Part = paste0("Median: &chi;<sup>2</sup> = ", sprintf("%.2f", Chi_value), 
                      " (*p* ", ifelse(p_int < 0.001, "< 0.001*", paste0("= ", sprintf("%.3f", p_int), ifelse(p_int < 0.05, "*", ""))), ")"),
    Var_Part = paste0("Variance: F = ", sprintf("%.2f", F_value), 
                      " (*p* ", ifelse(p_lv < 0.001, "< 0.001*", paste0("= ", sprintf("%.3f", p_lv), ifelse(p_lv < 0.05, "*", ""))), ")"),
    Test_Summary = paste0(Med_Part, " &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp; ", Var_Part),
    Group_Label = paste0("**", Classifier, "**<br>",
                         "***", Dimension, "***<br>",
                         "<span style='font-size: 85%'>", Test_Summary, "</span>"),
    Basin = as.character(Basin)) %>%
  left_join(posthoc_ref2, by = c("Dimension", "Basin")) %>%
  mutate(PostHoc_Summary = coalesce(PostHoc_Summary, "&mdash;"))
  


postPCA_geo2_table <- geo2_results_combined %>%
  select(
    Group_Label,
    Basin,
    `Amazon molly`,
    `Sailfin molly`,
    PostHoc_Summary) %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "Group_Label", process_md = TRUE) %>%
  fmt_markdown(columns = everything()) %>%
  cols_label(
    Basin = " ",
    `Amazon molly` = md("**Amazon**<br>Median (MAD)"),
    `Sailfin molly` = md("**Sailfin**<br>Median (MAD)"),
    PostHoc_Summary = md("**Post-hoc Contrasts**<br>(M)edian / (V)ariance<br><span style='font-size:90%; font-weight:normal;'>est. (SE); adj. p</span>")) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(sides = "right", color = "lightgray", weight = px(1.5)), 
    locations = cells_body(columns = c(Basin, `Amazon molly`, `Sailfin molly`))) %>%
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = everything()) %>%
  opt_horizontal_padding(scale = 2)

#final table
postPCA_geo2_table





```
***
***

## **Tampico MX Analysis**

For this analysis, we will compare sailfin, amazon and atlantic mollies from the Tampico, Mexico region. This is the proposed origin of Amazon mollies, and the only region in which all three species coexist. 

After filtering for individuals where the county/state information was recorded as Tamaulipas/MX, we have 32 sailfin molly, 28 Amazon molly, and 32 Atlantic molly.

### Individual trait models

**CONCLUSIONS:** Body depth, predorsal length, and dorsal base length were best supported with an unequal variance model; in all cases, the sexual species (sailfin & atlantic) were more variable than the amazon. All remaining metric traits and all meristic traits were best supported with an equal variance model (aka no significant difference in variation among the species). 


```{r}
#create separate df for metric and meristic (count) data
metric3 <- geo %>%
  select(-c(5:12)) %>%
  select(-c("FLA"))

count3 <- geo %>%
  select(-c(14:23)) #still includes SL for analysis



# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## METRIC ANALYSIS 
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Ensure Species is a factor
metric3$Species <- as.factor(metric3$Species)
metric3$Species <- relevel(metric3$Species, ref = "Amazon molly")

tm_results_list <- lapply(met.vars, function(trait) {
  
  # Build the formula string correctly
  form_str <- paste0("log(", trait, ") ~ log(SL) * Species")
  form <- as.formula(form_str)

  # 1. Homogeneous variance model (with safety catch)
  mod1 <- tryCatch({
    glmmTMB(form, data = metric3, family = gaussian)
  }, error = function(e) { NULL }, warning = function(w) { NULL })
  
  # 2. Heterogeneous variance model (with strict optimization catching)
  mod2 <- tryCatch({
    glmmTMB(form, data = metric3, family = gaussian, dispformula = ~Species)
  }, 
  error = function(e) { NULL }, 
  warning = function(w) { 
    if(grepl("NA/NaN|calc_loglambda|iteration|failure", w$message, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
      return(NULL) 
    }
    invokeRestart("muffleWarning") 
  })
  
  # Verify clean convergence of both models
  models_valid <- !is.null(mod1) && !is.null(mod2) && 
                  !is.na(AIC(mod1)) && !is.na(AIC(mod2)) && 
                  !any(is.nan(summary(mod2)$coefficients$disp[,2]))

  unique_species <- levels(as.factor(metric3$Species))
  
  # --- PATH A: MODEL CONVERGED CLEANLY ---
  if (models_valid) {
    # Compare models
    comp <- anova(mod1, mod2)
    pval <- comp$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2]
    delta_aic <- AIC(mod2) - AIC(mod1)
    
    # Run DHARMa residual check
    best_mod <- if (AIC(mod1) < AIC(mod2)) mod1 else mod2
    best_mod_name <- if (AIC(mod1) < AIC(mod2)) "Homogeneous (mod1)" else "Heterogeneous (mod2)"
    
    message(paste0("--- DHARMa Diagnostics for Trait: ", trait, " (Best Model: ", best_mod_name, ") ---"))
    sim_res <- simulateResiduals(best_mod, plot = FALSE)
    
    # Plot the residuals automatically
    plot(sim_res) 
    title(main = paste(trait, "-", best_mod_name), line = 2.5)
    
    # Extract predicted log-variance per species
    rg <- ref_grid(mod2, component = "disp")
    disp_means <- as.data.frame(emmeans(rg, specs = ~ Species))
    disp_means$Variance <- exp(disp_means$emmean)
    
    # Pivot to easily calculate the single global ratio
    ratio_df <- disp_means %>%
      select(Species, Variance) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = Species, values_from = Variance) %>%
      mutate(
        SA_Ratio  = `Sailfin molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        AA_Ratio  = `Atlantic molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        Delta_AIC  = delta_aic, 
        LRT_p      = pval,
        Best_Model = best_mod_name)
      
  } else {
    # --- PATH B: FALLBACK TO SIZE-CORRECTED LEVENE'S TEST ---
    best_mod_name <- "Model Failed - Levene's Test Fallback"
    message(paste0("!!! glmmTMB Failed for Trait: ", trait, ". Running Levene's Fallback. !!!"))
    
    # Step 1: Strip out body size dependencies using linear model on log traits
    lm_check <- lm(as.formula(paste0("log(", trait, ") ~ log(SL)")), data = metric3)
    metric$Abs_Resid <- abs(residuals(lm_check))
    
    # Step 2: Run a formal Levene's-style test via anova on absolute deviations
    lev_test <- tryCatch({
      leveneTest(Abs_Resid ~ Species, data = metric3)
    }, error = function(e) { NULL })
    fallback_p <- if(!is.null(lev_test)) lev_test$`Pr(>F)`[1] else NA
    
    # Step 3: Calculate empirical size-corrected variances per group
    empirical_vars <- metric3 %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(Variance = var(log(!!sym(trait)), na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop') %>%
      complete(Species = unique_species, fill = list(Variance = NA))
    
    ratio_df <- empirical_vars %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = Species, values_from = Variance) %>%
      mutate(
        SA_Ratio  = `Sailfin molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        AA_Ratio  = `Atlantic molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        Delta_AIC  = NA, # Marked NA so it shows as blank/dash in your table
        LRT_p      = fallback_p,
        Best_Model = best_mod_name)
  }
  
  # Append baseline trait tracker string
  ratio_df$Trait <- trait
  return(ratio_df)
})

tam_results_df <- do.call(rbind, tam_results_list)

tam_results_df$p_adj <- p.adjust(tam_results_df$LRT_p,
                             method = "holm")

tam.met.results <- tam_results_df %>%
  mutate(
    p.display = case_when(
      p_adj < 0.001 ~ paste0("*p* < 0.001*"),
      p_adj < 0.05  ~ paste0(sprintf("%.3f", p_adj), "*"),
      TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", p_adj))) %>%
  mutate(
    Trait_Name = factor(
      trait.names[Trait],
      levels = unname(trait.names))) %>%
  arrange(Trait_Name) %>%
  relocate(Trait_Name, .before = everything())

tam.met.tbl <- tam.met.results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_hide(c(Trait, LRT_p, p_adj, "Sailfin molly", "Amazon molly", "Atlantic molly")) %>%
  cols_label(
    Trait_Name = "Trait",
    SA_Ratio = md("Variance ratio <br>(Sailfin / Amazon)"),
    AA_Ratio = md("Variance ratio <br>(Atlantic / Amazon)"),
    Delta_AIC = md("&Delta;AIC"),
    p.display = md("*p*-value")) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(SA_Ratio, AA_Ratio, Delta_AIC),
    decimals = 2) %>%
  fmt_markdown(columns = p.display) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(sides = "right", color = "lightgray", weight = px(1.5)),
    locations = cells_body(columns = c(Trait_Name, Delta_AIC, SA_Ratio, AA_Ratio))) %>%
  cols_align("center", everything()) %>%
  opt_horizontal_padding(scale = 2)
  
  
tam.met.tbl



# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## MERISTIC (COUNT) ANALYSIS 
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
### NOTE: issues with convergence since variation is so small and data is truncated

tam_results_list_count <- lapply(cnt.vars, function(trait) {
  
  form_str <- paste0(trait, " ~ SL * Species")
  form <- as.formula(form_str)
  
  # Initialize status tracking flags
  mod2_failed <- FALSE
  levene_failed <- FALSE
  
  # 1. Fit Baseline Homogeneous Model
  mod1 <- tryCatch({
    glmmTMB(form, data = count3, family = compois)
  }, error = function(e) { NULL }, warning = function(w) { NULL }) # Rejects if optimizer warns
  
  # 2. Fit Heterogeneous Model (with strict warning/error catching)
  mod2 <- tryCatch({
    glmmTMB(form, data = count3, family = compois, dispformula = ~Species)
  }, 
  error = function(e) { NULL }, 
  warning = function(w) { 
    # Catch internal optimization/evaluation warnings and treat them as failures
    if(grepl("NA/NaN|calc_loglambda|iteration|failure", w$message, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
      return(NULL) 
    }
    # Allow minor, harmless warnings to pass through
    invokeRestart("muffleWarning") 
  })

  # Verify if both models successfully and cleanly converged
  models_valid <- !is.null(mod1) && !is.null(mod2) && 
                  !is.na(AIC(mod1)) && !is.na(AIC(mod2)) && 
                  !any(is.nan(summary(mod2)$coefficients$disp[,2]))

  unique_species <- levels(as.factor(count3$Species))
  
  # --- PATH A: GLMMTMB CONVERGED CLEANLY ---
  if (models_valid) {
    comp <- anova(mod1, mod2)
    pval <- comp$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2]
    delta_aic <- AIC(mod2) - AIC(mod1)
    
    best_mod <- if (AIC(mod1) < AIC(mod2)) mod1 else mod2
    best_mod_name <- if (AIC(mod1) < AIC(mod2)) "Homogeneous (mod1)" else "Heterogeneous (mod2)"
    
    # Run DHARMa diagnostics safely
    message(paste0("--- DHARMa Diagnostics for Trait: ", trait, " (Best Model: ", best_mod_name, ") ---"))
    sim_res <- simulateResiduals(best_mod, plot = FALSE)
    plot(sim_res) 
    title(main = paste(trait, "-", best_mod_name), line = 2.5)
    
    # Extract Model Variances
    mean_sl <- mean(count3$SL, na.rm = TRUE)
    pred_data <- data.frame(Species = unique_species, SL = mean_sl)
    pred_data$Mean <- predict(mod2, newdata = pred_data, type = "response")
    pred_data$Phi  <- exp(predict(mod2, newdata = pred_data, type = "disp"))
    pred_data$Variance <- pred_data$Mean * pred_data$Phi
    
    output_df <- pred_data %>%
      select(Species, Variance) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = Species, values_from = Variance) %>%
      mutate(
        SA_Ratio   = `Sailfin molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        AA_Ratio   = `Atlantic molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        Delta_AIC  = delta_aic,
        LRT_p      = pval,
        Best_Model = best_mod_name)
      
  } else {
    # --- PATH B: CONVERGENCE FAILED -> FALLBACK TO LEVENE'S TEST ---
    best_mod_name <- "Model Failed Optimization - Used Levene's Test Fallback"
    message(paste0("!!! glmmTMB Failed for Trait: ", trait, ". Running Levene's Test Fallback. !!!"))
    
    # Run Levene's Test on the raw response variable across species
    levene_form <- as.formula(paste0(trait, " ~ Species"))
    lev_test <- tryCatch({
      leveneTest(levene_form, data = count3)
    }, error = function(e) { NULL })
    
    # Extract empirical variances directly from the raw data
    raw_vars <- count3 %>%
      group_by(Species) %>%
      summarise(Variance = var(!!sym(trait), na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop') %>%
      complete(Species = unique_species, fill = list(Variance = NA)) # handle missing classes
    
    # Calculate ratios using the empirical data
    wide_vars <- raw_vars %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = Species, values_from = Variance)
    
    # Pull p-value from Levene's test table
    l_p <- if(!is.null(lev_test)) lev_test$`Pr(>F)`[1] else NA
    
    output_df <- wide_vars %>%
      mutate(
        SA_Ratio   = `Sailfin molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        AA_Ratio   = `Atlantic molly` / `Amazon molly`,
        Delta_AIC  = NA,   # AIC is invalid because the likelihood engine failed
        LRT_p      = l_p,  # Storing Levene's p-value here so it seamlessly passes into p_adj
        Best_Model = best_mod_name)
  }
  
  # Append the raw trait identifier string
  output_df$Trait <- trait
  return(output_df)
})


# Combine into a clean data frame
tam_cnt_df <- do.call(rbind, tam_results_list_count)

tam_cnt_df$p_adj <- p.adjust(tam_cnt_df$LRT_p,
                             method = "holm")

tam.cnt.results <- tam_cnt_df %>%
  mutate(
    p.display = case_when(
      p_adj < 0.001 ~ paste0("*p* < 0.001*"),
      p_adj < 0.05  ~ paste0(sprintf("%.3f", p_adj), "*"),
      TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", p_adj))) %>%
  mutate(
    Trait_Name = factor(
      trait.names[Trait],
      levels = unname(trait.names))) %>%
  arrange(Trait_Name) %>%
  relocate(Trait_Name, .before = everything())

tam.cnt.tbl <- tam.cnt.results %>%
  gt() %>%
  cols_hide(c(Trait, LRT_p, p_adj, "Sailfin molly", "Amazon molly", "Atlantic molly")) %>%
  cols_label(
    Trait_Name = "Trait",
    SA_Ratio = md("Variance ratio <br>(Sailfin / Amazon)"),
    AA_Ratio = md("Variance ratio <br>(Atlantic / Amazon)"),
    Delta_AIC = md("&Delta;AIC"),
    p.display = md("*p*-value")) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(SA_Ratio, AA_Ratio, Delta_AIC),
    decimals = 2) %>%
  fmt_markdown(columns = p.display) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(sides = "right", color = "lightgray", weight = px(1.5)),
    locations = cells_body(columns = c(Trait_Name, Delta_AIC, SA_Ratio, AA_Ratio))) %>%
  cols_align("center", everything()) %>%
  opt_horizontal_padding(scale = 2)
  
  
tam.cnt.tbl

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## PLOTS
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

met.long2 <- metric3 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(SL, BD, CPD, CPL, PreDL, DbL, HL, HD, HW, SnL, OL),
    names_to = "Trait",
    values_to = "Value")


tam.met.plots <- ggplot(met.long2, aes(x=Species, y=Value, fill = Species)) + 
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_point(color= "#8D8680", size=2, alpha=0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Trait, scales = "free_y", axes = "margins") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 10, base_family = "sans") + 
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"), 
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"),
    strip.text    = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.5, "lines")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Sailfin molly"="#78B7C5",
                               "Amazon molly"="#E1AF00",
                               "Atlantic molly"="#9986A5")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Sailfin molly" = "Sailfin\nmolly", 
                              "Amazon molly" = "Amazon\nmolly", 
                              "Atlantic molly" = "Atlantic\nmolly")) +
  xlab("") +
  ylab("Length or Depth (mm)")
tam.met.plots


cnt.long2 <- count3 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(D, P1, A, P1.R, LLSC, SALL, SBLL, SBDF, FLA),
    names_to = "Trait",
    values_to = "Value")

tam.cnt.plots <- ggplot(cnt.long2, aes(x=Species, y=Value, fill = Species)) + 
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_point(color= "#8D8680", size=2, alpha=0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Trait, scales = "free_y", axes = "margins") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 10, base_family = "sans") + 
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"), 
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"),
    strip.text    = element_text(size = 10, family = "sans"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.5, "lines")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Sailfin molly"="#78B7C5",
                               "Amazon molly"="#E1AF00",
                               "Atlantic molly"="#9986A5")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Sailfin molly" = "Sailfin\nmolly", 
                              "Amazon molly" = "Amazon\nmolly", 
                              "Atlantic molly" = "Atlantic\nmolly")) +
  xlab("") +
  ylab("Count")
tam.cnt.plots


```

***
***

### Overall shape variation (PCA)

As before, we will assess overall shape variation among species using a ***principal component analysis (PCA)*** on centered and scaled data (all metric traits will also be log transformed to better approach normality). We will then perform a mean and variance test (e.g., ANOVA and Levene's test) to determine if overall shape variation differs between the species. 

As meristic traits are count data, a normal PCA cannot be applied. Instead, we will use a ***categorical principal component analysis (CATPCA)*** that uses optimal scaling to deal with discrete integers. 

#### *Loadings*

```{r}
#log transform metric traits
df_trans2 <- geo
df_trans2[, c(13:23)] <- log(df_trans2[, c(13:23)])

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## METRIC ANALYSIS 
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────


PCA.test2 <- prcomp(df_trans2[, 13:23], center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE) 

summary(PCA.test2)
(eig.val.test <- get_eigenvalue(PCA.test2))


# For many morphological studies, the first component is heavily weighted by body size and is often of less interest than components two and three (Humphries et al., 1981). Therefore, we will extract the first three dimensions

ind.test <- get_pca_ind(PCA.test2)
head(ind.test$coord[,1:3])


pca.df2 <- cbind(df_trans2, ind.test$coord[,1:3])


# Will investigate the influence of each trait on the first 3 dimensions
(loadings <- PCA.test2$rotation[, 1:3])

sorted.loadings.1 <- loadings[order(loadings[, 1]), 1]
myTitle <- "Loadings Plot for PC1" 
myXlab  <- "Variable Loadings"
dotchart(sorted.loadings.1, main=myTitle, xlab=myXlab, cex=0.5, col="blue")

sorted.loadings.2 <- loadings[order(loadings[, 2]), 2]
myTitle <- "Loadings Plot for PC2" 
myXlab  <- "Variable Loadings"
dotchart(sorted.loadings.2, main=myTitle, xlab=myXlab, cex=0.5, col="blue")

sorted.loadings.3 <- loadings[order(loadings[, 3]), 3]
myTitle <- "Loadings Plot for PC3" 
myXlab  <- "Variable Loadings"
dotchart(sorted.loadings.3, main=myTitle, xlab=myXlab, cex=0.5, col="blue")




# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## MERISTIC (COUNT) ANALYSIS 
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Remove SL from count df
count4 <- select(count3, -c("SL"))

# Run the Categorical PCA
# 'ndim = 2' extracts the first two principal components
# 'degrees = 1' treats the scaling transformations as piecewise linear (ideal for ordinal integers)

catpca.test2 <- princals(count4[, 5:13], ndim = 2, degrees = 1)

# Print the summary to see how much variance your new components explain
summary(catpca.test2)

(cat.loadings <- catpca.test2$loadings)

sorted.loadings.4 <- cat.loadings[order(cat.loadings[, 1]), 1]
myTitle <- "Loadings Plot for CATPC1" 
myXlab  <- "Variable Loadings"
dotchart(sorted.loadings.4, main=myTitle, xlab=myXlab, cex=0.5, col="blue")

sorted.loadings.5 <- cat.loadings[order(cat.loadings[, 2]), 2]
myTitle <- "Loadings Plot for CATPC2" 
myXlab  <- "Variable Loadings"
dotchart(sorted.loadings.5, main=myTitle, xlab=myXlab, cex=0.5, col="blue")

# Extract the eigenvalues (one per dimension)
eigenvals <- catpca.test2$evals

# Calculate the percentage of variation explained by each axis
# (Divided by 9 because you have 9 meristic traits)
pc1_percent <- round((eigenvals[1] / 9) * 100, 1)
pc2_percent <- round((eigenvals[2] / 9) * 100, 1)

# Quick print check to see your numbers (e.g., 24.5 and 14.2)
print(paste0("CatPC1: ", pc1_percent, "%"))
print(paste0("CatPC2: ", pc2_percent, "%"))

# 1. Extract the object scores for the first two dimensions
cat_pc_scores <- as.data.frame(catpca.test2$objectscores)
colnames(cat_pc_scores) <- c("CatPC1", "CatPC2")

# 2. Combine them back with your species and size metadata
catpca.df2 <- cbind(count4, cat_pc_scores)

```

#### *Plots*

```{r}

#### METRIC

tam.pca.1<- autoplot(PCA.test2, data = df_trans2, colour="Species", shape= 'Species', loadings=FALSE, loadings.label=FALSE, frame=TRUE, frame.type='norm', label= FALSE)+
  scale_color_manual(name="Species",
                    values = c("Sailfin molly"="#78B7C5", "Amazon molly"="#E1AF00", "Atlantic molly"="#9986A5")) +
  scale_fill_manual(name="Species",
                    values = c("Sailfin molly"="#78B7C5", "Amazon molly"="#E1AF00", "Atlantic molly"="#9986A5")) +
  scale_shape_manual(name= 'Species', 
                     values = c("Sailfin molly" = 17, "Amazon molly"=16, "Atlantic molly"= 3)) +
   guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(fill = NA)))+
  ggtitle("PCA Plot of Morphology traits") + theme_classic() 
tam.pca.1


tam.pca.2<- autoplot(PCA.test2, x=2, y=3, data = df_trans2, colour="Species", shape= 'Species', loadings=FALSE, loadings.label=FALSE, frame=TRUE, frame.type='norm', label= FALSE)+
  scale_color_manual(name="Species",
                    values = c("Sailfin molly"="#78B7C5", "Amazon molly"="#E1AF00", "Atlantic molly"="#9986A5")) +
  scale_fill_manual(name="Species",
                    values = c("Sailfin molly"="#78B7C5", "Amazon molly"="#E1AF00", "Atlantic molly"="#9986A5")) +
  scale_shape_manual(name= 'Species', 
                     values = c("Sailfin molly" = 17, "Amazon molly"=16, "Atlantic molly"= 3)) +
   guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(fill = NA)))+
  ggtitle("PCA Plot of Morphology traits") + theme_classic() 
tam.pca.2


#### MERISTIC

tam.catpca <- ggplot(catpca.df2, aes(x = CatPC1, y = CatPC2, color = Species, fill = Species, shape = Species)) +
  geom_point(size = 1, alpha = 0.8) +
  stat_ellipse(geom = "polygon", alpha = 0.15, type = "norm", level = 0.95) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Species",
                     values = c("Sailfin molly" = "#78B7C5", "Amazon molly" = "#E1AF00", "Atlantic molly"="#9986A5")) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Species",
                    values = c("Sailfin molly" = "#78B7C5", "Amazon molly" = "#E1AF00", "Atlantic molly"="#9986A5")) +
  scale_shape_manual(name = "Species", 
                     values = c("Sailfin molly" = 17, "Amazon molly" = 16, "Atlantic molly"= 3)) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(fill = NA))) +
  labs(
    x = "Categorical PC1",
    y = "Categorical PC2"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = paste0("Categorical PC1 (", pc1_percent, "%)"),
    y = paste0("Categorical PC2 (", pc2_percent, "%)")
  )

tam.catpca

```


#### *Post PCA tests*

**CONCLUSIONS:** For our metric data, there is no significant difference in average shape distribution for PC1 or PC3, but there is a significant difference for PC2. This is expected, as all three species have different body shapes and therefore occupy distinct morphospace. PC1 is influenced by body size, so PC2 and PC3 are of more interest. The species occupy a different morphospace for PC2 which explains 27.1% of the shape variation, but occupy similar morphospace for PC3 which only explains 4.48%. 

PC1 and PC2 both show a significant difference in variation, with pochoc tests revealing the sailfin-Atlantic comparisons to be driving this. Some of the traits that load heavily on PC1 and PC2 (e.g. body depth, dorsal base length) are also the traits that were best supported by unequal variances in our individual models, with the sexual species again having more variation than the asexual, so the PCA results seem to corroborate this. This indicates that for both individual traits and when traits are taken all together, the sexual species in Tampico, Mexico are more variable than the asexual species.


For our meristic data, both CatPC1 and CatPC2 showed a significant difference in average meristic profiles, which again makes sense as many of the meristic traits are species specific. There is also a significant difference in variation for CatPC1, with the pairwise comparisons showing a significant difference for sailfin and atlantic only; there was no significant difference in CatPC2. This differs from our individual trait analysis, indicating species differences in covariation among multiple meristic features. 

```{r}
#quick test

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## MEDIAN TESTS 
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

### Metric Data

tam.met.mean.1 <- glmmTMB(Dim.1~Species, data = pca.df2) 
(test1<- Anova(tam.met.mean.1, type = "III")) #significant (p=0.008)
plot(simulateResiduals(tam.met.mean.1)) #fails levene's test

    test.ph <- emmeans(tam.met.mean.1,  ~ Species)
    summary(test.ph)
    test.pw <- pairs(test.ph)
    (pair.test <- as.data.frame(rbind(test.pw, adjust = "holm"))) #only amazon and atlantic sig (p=0.008)
    
tam.met.mean.2 <- glmmTMB(Dim.2~Species, data = pca.df2) #not significant
(test2<- Anova(tam.met.mean.2, type = "III")) #significant (p<0.001)
plot(simulateResiduals(tam.met.mean.2)) #all good
    post_hocTEST <- emmeans(tam.met.mean.1,  ~ Species)
    summary(post_hocTEST)
    pwTEST <- pairs(post_hocTEST)
    (pairTEST <- as.data.frame(rbind(pwTEST, adjust = "holm"))) 


tam.met.mean.3 <- kruskal.test(Dim.3~Species, data = pca.df2) #not significant


### Meristic Data


tam.cnt.mean.1 <- kruskal.test(CatPC1~Species, data = catpca.df2) #significant (p<0.001)
    tam_post_hoc2 <- dunnTest(CatPC1 ~ Species, data = catpca.df2, method = "holm")
    tam_post_hoc2 #all comparisons significant (p<0.001)

tam.cnt.mean.2 <- kruskal.test(CatPC2~Species, data = catpca.df2) #significant (p=0.009)
    tam_post_hoc3 <- dunnTest(CatPC2 ~ Species, data = catpca.df2, method = "holm")
    tam_post_hoc3 #amazon-atlantic (p=0.0.019) and sailfin-atlatnic (p=0.021) significant but not amazon-sailfin

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## VARIANCE TESTS (Brown-Forsythe for all)
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

### Metric Data
tam.met.var.1 <- leveneTest(Dim.1~Species, data= pca.df2) #significant (p=0.016)

    tam_post_hoc4 <- with(pca.df2, {
      medians <- tapply(Dim.1, Species, median, na.rm = TRUE)
      abs_devs <- abs(Dim.1 - medians[Species])
      pairwise.t.test(abs_devs, Species, p.adjust.method = "holm", pool.sd = FALSE)})
    tam_post_hoc4 #amazon-sailfin marginal (p=0.056), sailfin-atlantic significant (p=0.009) and amazon-atlatnic not significant


tam.met.var.2 <- leveneTest(Dim.2~Species, data= pca.df2) #significant (p=0.005)
    tam_post_hoc5 <- with(pca.df2, {
      medians <- tapply(Dim.2, Species, median, na.rm = TRUE)
      abs_devs <- abs(Dim.2 - medians[Species])
      pairwise.t.test(abs_devs, Species, p.adjust.method = "holm", pool.sd = FALSE)})
    tam_post_hoc5 #amazon-atlantic marginal (p=0.049), sailfin-atlantic significant (p=0.012) and amazon-sailfin not significant

tam.met.var.3 <- leveneTest(Dim.3~Species, data= pca.df2) #not significant


### Meristic Data
tam.cnt.var.1 <- leveneTest(CatPC1~Species, data= catpca.df2) #significant (p=0.002)
    tam_post_hoc6 <- with(catpca.df2, {
      medians <- tapply(CatPC1, Species, median, na.rm = TRUE)
      abs_devs <- abs(CatPC1 - medians[Species])
      pairwise.t.test(abs_devs, Species, p.adjust.method = "holm", pool.sd = FALSE)})
    tam_post_hoc6 #sailfin-atlantic significant (p=0.004) and amazon-sailfin and amazon-atlantic not significant

tam.cnt.var.2 <- leveneTest(CatPC2~Species, data= catpca.df2) #not significant




```



```{r}

#quick distribution checks to see what tests to use

testmod <- lm(Dim.1 ~ Species, data = pca.df2)
plot(simulateResiduals(testmod)) #fails levene's test; will use kruskal-wallis

testmod <- lm(Dim.2 ~ Species, data = pca.df2)
plot(simulateResiduals(testmod)) #fails levene's test; will use kruskal-wallis

testmod <- lm(Dim.3 ~ Species, data = pca.df2)
plot(simulateResiduals(testmod)) #all good

testmod <- lm(CatPC1 ~ Species, data = catpca.df2)
plot(simulateResiduals(testmod)) #fails outlier and levene's test; will use kruskal-wallis

testmod <- lm(CatPC2 ~ Species, data = catpca.df2)
plot(simulateResiduals(testmod)) #fails KS test and outlier test; will use kruskal-wallis




# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## MEDIAN TESTS (Kruskal-Wallis test for all)
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

### Metric Data

tam.met.mean.1 <- kruskal.test(Dim.1~Species, data = pca.df2) #not significant

tam.met.mean.2 <- kruskal.test(Dim.2~Species, data = pca.df2) #significant (p<0.001)
    tam_post_hoc1 <- dunnTest(Dim.2 ~ Species, data = pca.df2, method = "holm")
    tam_post_hoc1 #all comparisons significant (p<0.001)


tam.met.mean.3 <- kruskal.test(Dim.3~Species, data = pca.df2) #not significant


### Meristic Data


tam.cnt.mean.1 <- kruskal.test(CatPC1~Species, data = catpca.df2) #significant (p<0.001)
    tam_post_hoc2 <- dunnTest(CatPC1 ~ Species, data = catpca.df2, method = "holm")
    tam_post_hoc2 #all comparisons significant (p<0.001)

tam.cnt.mean.2 <- kruskal.test(CatPC2~Species, data = catpca.df2) #significant (p=0.009)
    tam_post_hoc3 <- dunnTest(CatPC2 ~ Species, data = catpca.df2, method = "holm")
    tam_post_hoc3 #amazon-atlantic (p=0.0.019) and sailfin-atlatnic (p=0.021) significant but not amazon-sailfin

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## VARIANCE TESTS (Brown-Forsythe for all)
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

### Metric Data
tam.met.var.1 <- leveneTest(Dim.1~Species, data= pca.df2) #significant (p=0.016)

    tam_post_hoc4 <- with(pca.df2, {
      medians <- tapply(Dim.1, Species, median, na.rm = TRUE)
      abs_devs <- abs(Dim.1 - medians[Species])
      pairwise.t.test(abs_devs, Species, p.adjust.method = "holm", pool.sd = FALSE)})
    tam_post_hoc4 #amazon-sailfin marginal (p=0.056), sailfin-atlantic significant (p=0.009) and amazon-atlatnic not significant


tam.met.var.2 <- leveneTest(Dim.2~Species, data= pca.df2) #significant (p=0.005)
    tam_post_hoc5 <- with(pca.df2, {
      medians <- tapply(Dim.2, Species, median, na.rm = TRUE)
      abs_devs <- abs(Dim.2 - medians[Species])
      pairwise.t.test(abs_devs, Species, p.adjust.method = "holm", pool.sd = FALSE)})
    tam_post_hoc5 #amazon-atlantic marginal (p=0.049), sailfin-atlantic significant (p=0.012) and amazon-sailfin not significant

tam.met.var.3 <- leveneTest(Dim.3~Species, data= pca.df2) #not significant


### Meristic Data
tam.cnt.var.1 <- leveneTest(CatPC1~Species, data= catpca.df2) #significant (p=0.002)
    tam_post_hoc6 <- with(catpca.df2, {
      medians <- tapply(CatPC1, Species, median, na.rm = TRUE)
      abs_devs <- abs(CatPC1 - medians[Species])
      pairwise.t.test(abs_devs, Species, p.adjust.method = "holm", pool.sd = FALSE)})
    tam_post_hoc6 #sailfin-atlantic significant (p=0.004) and amazon-sailfin and amazon-atlantic not significant

tam.cnt.var.2 <- leveneTest(CatPC2~Species, data= catpca.df2) #not significant


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
## TABLE
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

### Metric Data

#get mean and variance for each dimension per species
met_summary2 <- pca.df2 %>%
  group_by(Species) %>%
  summarise(
    PC1_median = median(Dim.1, na.rm = TRUE),
    PC1_mad  = mad(Dim.1, na.rm = TRUE, constant = 1),
    PC2_median = median(Dim.2, na.rm = TRUE),
    PC2_mad  = mad(Dim.2, na.rm = TRUE, constant = 1),
    PC3_median = median(Dim.3, na.rm = TRUE),
    PC3_mad  = mad(Dim.3, na.rm = TRUE, constant = 1))

#isolate results
amazon_met  <- filter(met_summary2, Species == "Amazon molly")
sailfin_met <- filter(met_summary2, Species == "Sailfin molly")
atlantic_met <- filter(met_summary2, Species == "Atlantic molly")

#extract wilcox test results, levene's results, and summary stats
tam_postPCA_result <- tibble(
  Classifier = "Metric PCA",
  Dimension = c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"),
  Amazon = c(
    sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", amazon_met$PC1_median, amazon_met$PC1_mad),
    sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", amazon_met$PC2_median, amazon_met$PC2_mad),
    sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", amazon_met$PC3_median, amazon_met$PC3_mad)),
  Sailfin = c(
    sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", sailfin_met$PC1_median, sailfin_met$PC1_mad),
    sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", sailfin_met$PC2_median, sailfin_met$PC2_mad),
    sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", sailfin_met$PC3_median, sailfin_met$PC3_mad)),
  Atlantic = c(
    sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", atlantic_met$PC1_median, atlantic_met$PC1_mad),
    sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", atlantic_met$PC2_median, atlantic_met$PC2_mad),
    sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", atlantic_met$PC3_median, atlantic_met$PC3_mad)),
  H_value = c(tam.met.mean.1$statistic,
              tam.met.mean.2$statistic,
              tam.met.mean.3$statistic),
  p_kw = c(tam.met.mean.1$p.value,
           tam.met.mean.2$p.value,
           tam.met.mean.3$p.value),
  F_value = c(tam.met.var.1$`F value`[1],
              tam.met.var.2$`F value`[1],
              tam.met.var.3$`F value`[1]),
  p_lv = c(tam.met.var.1$`Pr(>F)`[1],
           tam.met.var.2$`Pr(>F)`[1],
           tam.met.var.3$`Pr(>F)`[1])) %>%
  mutate(
    Med_test = case_when(
      p_kw < 0.001 ~ paste0("H = ", sprintf("%.2f", H_value), 
                            "<br>*p* < 0.001*"),
      p_kw <0.05   ~ paste0("H = ", sprintf("%.2f", H_value), 
                            "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_kw), "*"),
       TRUE        ~ paste0("H = ", sprintf("%.2f", H_value),
                            "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_kw))),

    Variance_test = case_when(
      p_lv < 0.001 ~ paste0("F = ", sprintf("%.2f", F_value), 
                            "<br>*p* < 0.001*"),
      p_lv <0.05   ~ paste0("F = ", sprintf("%.2f", F_value), 
                            "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_lv), "*"),
       TRUE        ~ paste0("F = ", sprintf("%.2f", F_value),
                            "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_lv))))




### Meristic Data

#get mean and variance for each dimension per species
cnt_summary2 <- catpca.df2 %>%
  group_by(Species) %>%
  summarise(
    CPC1_median = median(CatPC1, na.rm = TRUE),
    CPC1_mad  = mad(CatPC1, na.rm = TRUE),
    CPC2_median = median(CatPC2, na.rm = TRUE),
    CPC2_mad  = mad(CatPC2, na.rm = TRUE))

#isolate results
amazon_cnt  <- filter(cnt_summary2, Species == "Amazon molly")
sailfin_cnt <- filter(cnt_summary2, Species == "Sailfin molly")
atlantic_cnt <- filter(cnt_summary2, Species == "Atlantic molly")


#extract wilcox test results, levene's results, and summary stats
tam_postCPCA_result <- tibble(
  Classifier = "Meristic CATPCA",
  Dimension = c("CatPC1", "CatPC2"),
  Amazon = c(
    sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", amazon_cnt$CPC1_median, amazon_cnt$CPC1_mad),
    sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", amazon_cnt$CPC2_median, amazon_cnt$CPC2_mad)),
  Sailfin = c(
    sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", sailfin_cnt$CPC1_median, sailfin_cnt$CPC1_mad),
    sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", sailfin_cnt$CPC2_median, sailfin_cnt$CPC2_mad)),
  Atlantic = c(
    sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", atlantic_cnt$CPC1_median, atlantic_cnt$CPC1_mad),
    sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", atlantic_cnt$CPC2_median, atlantic_cnt$CPC2_mad)),
  H_value = c(tam.cnt.mean.1$statistic,
              tam.cnt.mean.2$statistic),
  p_kw = c(tam.cnt.mean.1$p.value,
           tam.cnt.mean.2$p.value),
  F_value = c(tam.cnt.var.1$`F value`[1],
              tam.cnt.var.2$`F value`[1]),
  p_lv = c(tam.cnt.var.1$`Pr(>F)`[1],
           tam.cnt.var.2$`Pr(>F)`[1])) %>%
  mutate(
    Med_test = case_when(
      p_kw < 0.001 ~ paste0("H = ", sprintf("%.2f", H_value), 
                            "<br>*p* < 0.001*"),
      p_kw <0.05   ~ paste0("H = ", sprintf("%.2f", H_value), 
                            "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_kw), "*"),
       TRUE        ~ paste0("H = ", sprintf("%.2f", H_value),
                            "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_kw))),

    Variance_test = case_when(
      p_lv < 0.001 ~ paste0("F = ", sprintf("%.2f", F_value), 
                            "<br>*p* < 0.001*"),
      p_lv <0.05   ~ paste0("F = ", sprintf("%.2f", F_value), 
                            "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_lv), "*"),
       TRUE        ~ paste0("F = ", sprintf("%.2f", F_value),
                            "<br>*p* = ", sprintf("%.3f", p_lv))))




### Add posthoc test results
tam_extract_pairs <- function(pair_obj, dim_name, test.type = "M") {
  if (is.null(pair_obj)) return(NULL)
  
  # CASE A: If it's a Dunn Test object (FSA package for Medians)
  if (inherits(pair_obj, "dunnTest") || "res" %in% names(pair_obj)) {
    df <- pair_obj$res %>%
      rename(Contrast = Comparison, p.value = P.adj)
  } 
  # CASE B: If it's a pairwise.t.test object (Base R matrix for Variances)
  else if (inherits(pair_obj, "pairwise.htest")) {
    p_mat <- pair_obj$p.value
    df <- as.data.frame(as.table(p_mat)) %>%
      filter(!is.na(Freq)) %>%
      mutate(Contrast = paste0(Var2, " - ", Var1)) %>%
      rename(p.value = Freq)
  } else {
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  df %>%
    mutate(
      Dimension = dim_name,
      type = test.type,
      p_fmt = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "*p* < 0.001*",
        p.value < 0.05  ~ sprintf("*p* = %.3f*", p.value),
        TRUE            ~ sprintf("*p* = %.3f", p.value)),
      Contrast = gsub(" molly", "", Contrast), 
      PostHoc_Sum = sprintf("%s: %s", Contrast, p_fmt)) %>%
    select(Dimension, type, PostHoc_Sum)
}


posthoc_ref2 <- bind_rows(
  tam_extract_pairs(tam_post_hoc1, "PC2", "M"),
  tam_extract_pairs(tam_post_hoc2, "CatPC1", "M"),
  tam_extract_pairs(tam_post_hoc3, "CatPC2", "M"),
  tam_extract_pairs(tam_post_hoc4, "PC1", "V"),
  tam_extract_pairs(tam_post_hoc5, "PC2", "V"),
  tam_extract_pairs(tam_post_hoc6, "CatPC1", "V")) %>%
  group_by(Dimension, type) %>%
  summarise(
    Combined_Pairs = paste(PostHoc_Sum, collapse = "<br>"), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = type,
    values_from = Combined_Pairs,
    names_prefix = "PostHoc_")

### Combine & form table

tam_results_combined <- bind_rows(tam_postPCA_result, tam_postCPCA_result) %>%
  mutate(
    Med_Part = paste0("Median: H = ", sprintf("%.2f", H_value), 
                      " (*p* ", ifelse(p_kw < 0.001, "< 0.001*", paste0("= ", sprintf("%.3f", p_kw), ifelse(p_kw < 0.05, "*", ""))), ")"),
    Var_Part = paste0("Variance: F = ", sprintf("%.2f", F_value), 
                      " (*p* ", ifelse(p_lv < 0.001, "< 0.001*", paste0("= ", sprintf("%.3f", p_lv), ifelse(p_lv < 0.05, "*", ""))), ")"),
    Test_Summary = paste0(Med_Part, "<br> ", Var_Part),
    Group_Label = paste0("**", Classifier, "**<br>",
                         "***", Dimension, "***<br>",
                         "<span style='font-size: 85%'>", Test_Summary, "</span>")) %>%
  left_join(posthoc_ref2, by = "Dimension") %>%
  mutate(PostHoc_M = coalesce(PostHoc_M, "&mdash;"),
         PostHoc_V = coalesce(PostHoc_V, "&mdash;"))
  



tam_postPCA_table <- tam_results_combined %>%
  select(
    Group_Label,
    Amazon,
    Sailfin,
    Atlantic,
    PostHoc_M,
    PostHoc_V) %>%
  gt() %>%
  fmt_markdown(columns = everything()) %>%
  cols_label(
    Group_Label ="",
    Amazon = md("**Amazon**<br>Median (MAD)"),
    Sailfin = md("**Sailfin**<br>Median (MAD)"),
    Atlantic = md("**Atlantic**<br>Median (MAD)"),
    PostHoc_M = md("**Pairwise Medians**<br><span style='font-size:85%; font-weight:normal;'>Holm-adj. p</span>"),
    PostHoc_V = md("**Pairwise Variances**<br><span style='font-size:85%; font-weight:normal;'>Holm-adj. p</span>")) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(sides = "right", color = "lightgray", weight = px(1.5)), 
    locations = cells_body(columns = c(Group_Label, Amazon, Sailfin, Atlantic, PostHoc_M))) %>%
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = everything()) %>%
  opt_horizontal_padding(scale = 2)

#final table
tam_postPCA_table




```

***
***
